context: The agent must implement a jOOQ data access layer for an order-summary use case. The key instructions under test are: using Records.mapping() (not fetchInto(Dto.class)) for projected queries, matching column order to constructor parameter order, correctly applying the fetchInto exception for full-row fetches, and not creating test classes.

checklist.name: Records.mapping() for projection
checklist.description: The summary list query uses Records.mapping() (or fetch(Records.mapping(...))) rather than fetchInto(OrderSummaryDto.class) or fetchInto(OrderSummaryDto::new) with reflection

checklist.name: Constructor reference style
checklist.description: Records.mapping() is called with a constructor reference of the form OrderSummaryDto::new (not a lambda that manually constructs the DTO)

checklist.name: Column order matches constructor
checklist.description: The SELECT clause lists columns in the same order as the OrderSummaryDto constructor parameters: id first, then customer_name, then item count

checklist.name: Item count via aggregate
checklist.description: The summary query uses a COUNT() aggregate (e.g. DSL.count() or ORDER_ITEM.ID.count()) joined or correlated to ORDER_ITEM to produce the item count per order

checklist.name: fetchInto for full-row fetch
checklist.description: The single-order-by-ID method uses selectFrom(ORDER).fetchInto(...) or fetchOptionalInto(...) with the generated Order POJO — NOT Records.mapping() — consistent with the full-row exception

checklist.name: No fetchInto for projected query
checklist.description: The summary list method does NOT call fetchInto(OrderSummaryDto.class) anywhere

checklist.name: Sorted descending by created_at
checklist.description: The summary list query includes an ORDER BY clause on the order creation timestamp in descending order

checklist.name: No test classes created
checklist.description: No files ending in *Test.java or *IT.java are produced
